title: Towards a Science of AI Agent Reliability
abstract: Proposes metrics for consistency, robustness, predictability, and safety to evaluate how AI agents behave, degrade, and fail beyond a single aggregate success score.

title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
abstract: Evaluates LLM agents across multiple interactive environments and identifies common failure modes such as weak long-term reasoning, decision making, and instruction following.

title: Process Reward Models for LLM Agents: Practical Framework and Directions
abstract: Introduces AgentPRM and InversePRM for training LLM agents with process-level reward signals from rollouts or demonstrations, including analysis of reward shaping and reward hacking.

title: The Art of Building Verifiers for Computer Use Agents
abstract: Presents design lessons for computer-use-agent verifiers, including rubric construction, process and outcome rewards, failure categorization, and trajectory context management.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
abstract: Interleaves reasoning traces and actions for language-model agents, yielding interpretable trajectories that are useful for tool-use evaluation and verification.

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Uses verbal feedback and episodic memory to let agents reflect on task failures and improve subsequent attempts without model-weight updates.

title: Silent Failure in LLM Agent Systems: The Entropy Principle and the Inevitable Disorder of Autonomous Agents
abstract: Studies silent failures in LLM agent systems and frames reliability loss as an operational phenomenon requiring monitoring and deterministic governance.

title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains
abstract: Benchmarks tool-using agents in simulated user interactions with domain APIs and policy rules, introducing pass^k to measure reliability across repeated trials.

title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
abstract: Trains language models to decide when and how to call external APIs, forming an important foundation for later tool-agent reliability and verification work.

title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
abstract: Provides a realistic web environment and long-horizon tasks for measuring autonomous agent task completion and robustness in functional websites.
